info.title: FeatureScript Standard Library - Features-Modification
info.description: Body modification — boolean, chamfer, fillet, shell, draft, split, delete/move/replace faces

symbols.name: bodyDraft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: An operation that performs an `opBodyDraft`.

symbols.name: bodyDraftEditLogic
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, oldDefinition is map, definition is map, isCreating is boolean, specifiedParameters is map)
symbols.description: body draft editing logic

symbols.name: booleanBodies
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: The boolean feature. Performs an `opBoolean` after a possible `opOffsetFace` if the operation is subtraction.

symbols.name: convertNewBodyOpToBoolOp
symbols.signature: (operationType is NewBodyOperationType)
symbols.description: Maps a `NewBodyOperationType` (used in features like `extrude`) to its corresponding `BooleanOperationType`.

symbols.name: booleanStepTypePredicate
symbols.signature: (booleanDefinition is map)
symbols.description:
Predicate which specifies a field `operationType` of type `NewBodyOperationType`. Used by body-creating feature preconditions such as extrude, revolve, sweep or loft.
When used in a precondition, `NewBodyOperationType` creates UI like the extrude feature, with a horizontal list of the words "New", "Add", etc. When using this predicate in features, make sure to export an import of `tool.fs` so that `NewBodyOperationType` is visible to the Part Studios: 

```
export import(path : "onshape/std/tool.fs", version : "");
```

symbols.name: booleanStepScopePredicate
symbols.signature: (booleanDefinition is map)
symbols.description:
Used by body-creating feature preconditions to allow post-creation booleans, specifying the merge scope (or "Merge with all") for that boolean.
Designed to be used together with `booleanStepTypePredicate`.

symbols.name: booleanPatternScopePredicate
symbols.signature: (booleanDefinition is map)
symbols.description: Used by body-creating pattern feature preconditions to allow post-creation booleans with surfaces or solids, specifying the merge scope (or "Merge with all") for that boolean.

symbols.name: processNewBodyIfNeeded
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map, reconstructOp is function)
symbols.description: This function is designed to be used by body-creating features (like `extrude`) as a boolean post-processing step with options from `booleanStepTypePredicate` and `booleanStepScopePredicate` in the case where the preceding operations of the feature have created new solid or surface bodies. On top of the regular boolean operation, converts the `operationType` and creates error bodies on failure.

subfields.name: operationType
subfields.description:
Example: 
`NewBodyOperationType.ADD performs a boolean union` Example: 
`NewBodyOperationType.NEW does nothing`

subfields.name: defaultScope
subfields.description:
Optional Example: 
`true indicates merge scope of "everything else" (default)` Example: 
`false indicates merge scope is specified in booleanScope`

subfields.name: booleanScope
subfields.description: targets to use if `defaultScope` is false

subfields.name: seed
subfields.description: OptionalIf set, will be included in the tools section of the boolean.

subfields.name: mergeScopeExclusion
subfields.description: OptionalIf set, will be excluded from the targets section of the boolean.

parameters.name: reconstructOp
parameters.description:
A function which takes in an Id, and reconstructs the input to show to the user as error geometry in case the input is problematic or the boolean itself fails. 
 Example: 
`function() {} . For a more elaborate example see the source code of revolve feature in the Standard Library.`

symbols.name: surfaceOperationTypePredicate
symbols.signature: (surfaceDefinition is map)
symbols.description:
Predicate which specifies a field `surfaceOperationType` of type `NewSurfaceOperationType`. Used by surface-creating feature preconditions such as revolve, sweep or loft.
When used in a precondition, `NewSurfaceOperationType` creates UI like the sweep feature, with a horizontal list of the words "New" and "Add". When using this predicate in features, make sure to export an import of `tool.fs` so that `NewSurfaceOperationType` is visible to the Part Studios: 

```
export import(path : "onshape/std/tool.fs", version : "");
```

symbols.name: surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate
symbols.signature: (definition is map)
symbols.description:
Used by surface-creating feature preconditions to allow post-creation booleans, specifying the merge scope (or "Merge with all") for that boolean.
Designed to be used together with `surfaceOperationTypePredicate`.

symbols.name: joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map, makeSolid is boolean, reconstructOp is function)
symbols.description: This function is designed to be used by surface-body-creating features (like `extrude`) as a boolean post-processing step with options from `surfaceOperationTypePredicate` and `surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate`. It detects matching edges of adjacent bodies and joins surface bodies at these edges.

subfields.name: defaultSurfaceScope
subfields.description:
Optional Example: 
`true indicates merge scope of all the original and related surfaces used as input to create this surface (default)` Example: 
`false indicates merge scope is specified in booleanSurfaceScope`

subfields.name: booleanSurfaceScope
subfields.description: Optionaltargets to use if `defaultSurfaceScope` is false Default is `qNothing()`

subfields.name: seed
subfields.description: OptionalDefault is `qNothing()` If set, will be included in the tools section of the boolean.

parameters.name: makeSolid
parameters.description: Tries to join the surfaces into a solid

parameters.name: reconstructOp
parameters.description:
A function which takes in an Id, and reconstructs the input to show to the user as error geometry in case the input is problematic or the join itself fails. 
 Example: 
`function() {} . For a more elaborate example see the source code of revolve feature in the Standard Library.`

symbols.name: joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map, reconstructOp is function)
symbols.description: This function is designed to be used by surface-body-creating features (like `extrude`) as a boolean post-processing step with options from `surfaceOperationTypePredicate` and `surfaceJoinStepScopePredicate`. It detects matching edges of adjacent bodies and joins surface bodies at these edges.

subfields.name: defaultSurfaceScope
subfields.description:
Optional Example: 
`true indicates merge scope of all the original and related surfaces used as input to create this surface (default)` Example: 
`false indicates merge scope is specified in booleanSurfaceScope`

subfields.name: booleanSurfaceScope
subfields.description: Optionaltargets to use if `defaultSurfaceScope` is false Default is `qNothing()`

subfields.name: seed
subfields.description: OptionalDefault is `qNothing()` If set, will be included in the tools section of the boolean.

subfields.name: makeSolid
subfields.description: OptionalTries to join the surfaces into a solid Default is false

subfields.name: eraseImprintedEdges
subfields.description: OptionalMerge all mergeable imprinted edges created by the boolean operation Default is true

parameters.name: reconstructOp
parameters.description:
A function which takes in an Id, and reconstructs the input to show to the user as error geometry in case the input is problematic or the join itself fails. 
 Example: 
`function() {} . For a more elaborate example see the source code of revolve feature in the Standard Library.`

symbols.name: chamfer
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: The chamfer feature directly performs an `opChamfer` operation.

symbols.name: CompositePartDeleteOptions
symbols.description: Options that determine how the Delete part feature handles composite parts

values.name: DELETE
values.description: Any selected composite parts are deleted along with their constituents

values.name: DISSOLVE
values.description: Constituents of composites are only deleted if explicitly selected

symbols.name: deleteBodies
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Feature performing an `opDeleteBodies`.

symbols.name: DeleteFaceType
symbols.description: Specifies how the void resulting from delete face should be closed, if at all.

values.name: HEAL
values.description: Close void by shrinking or growing adjacent faces.

values.name: CAP
values.description: Close void by a simple surface passing through all edges.

values.name: VOID
values.description: Do not close the void. Creates surface out of solids.

symbols.name: deleteFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Feature performing an `opDeleteFace`. Has options to heal the void created by removing the selected faces, or to leave it open.

symbols.name: DraftFeatureType
symbols.description: Types of drafts available for the draft feature.

values.name: NEUTRAL_PLANE
values.description: Draft by holding the intersection between a set of faces and a neutral plane as a constant.

values.name: PARTING_LINE
values.description: Draft by holding a set of parting edges as a constant.

symbols.name: PartingLineSides
symbols.description: Specifies which faces to draft when drafting with DraftFeatureType.PARTING_LINE.

values.name: ONE_SIDED
values.description: Draft one of the faces attached to the parting line.

values.name: SYMMETRIC
values.description: Draft both of the faces attached to the parting line symmetrically.

values.name: TWO_SIDED
values.description: Draft both of the faces attached to the parting line with separate draft angles.

symbols.name: draft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Feature performing an `opDraft`.

subfields.name: draftFeatureType
subfields.description: OptionalSpecifies a `NEUTRAL_PLANE` or `PARTING_LINE` draft. Default is `NEUTRAL_PLANE`.

subfields.name: neutralPlane
subfields.description: Required if `draftFeatureType` is `NEUTRAL_PLANE` A planar face or mate connector defining both the neutral plane and pull direction of the the draft. The intersection of the drafted faces and the neutral plane remains unchanged. The pull direction of the draft will be the face normal or mate connector z-axis.

subfields.name: draftFaces
subfields.description: Required if `draftFeatureType` is `NEUTRAL_PLANE` The faces to draft for a `NEUTRAL_PLANE` draft.

subfields.name: pullDirectionEntity
subfields.description: Required if `draftFeatureType` is `PARTING_LINE` An entity defining the pull direction of the draft. This entity should conform to the `ALLOWS_DIRECTION` specification in `QueryFilterCompound`.

subfields.name: partingEdges
subfields.description: Required if `draftFeatureType` is `PARTING_LINE` Edges defining the parting line of the draft. These edges will remain unchanged as some adjacent faces, as defined by `partingLineSides`, are drafted.

subfields.name: hintFaces
subfields.description: OptionalFor Onshape internal use. For `PARTING_LINE` draft, specifies in advance which adjacent faces of `partingEdges` should be treated as more along the pull direction. When unspecified, the feature will use a geometric calculation to determine this distinction.

subfields.name: partingLineSides
subfields.description: Required if `draftFeatureType` is `PARTING_LINE` Specifies whether to draft one or both faces adjacent to the parting edges, and whether the draft should be symmetrical if drafting both faces. See `PartingLineSides`.

subfields.name: alongPull
subfields.description: Required if `PartingLineSides` is `ONE_SIDED` Specifies which face will be drafted in a `ONE_SIDED` `PARTING_LINE` draft. If `true`, the face which is more along the pull direction from the perspective of the parting edge is drafted. If `false`, the face which is more away from the pull direction from the perspective of the parting edge is drafted.

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description:
The draft angle, must be between 0 and 89.9 degrees. 
 Example: 
`3 * degree`

subfields.name: pullDirection
subfields.description: OptionalWhether the pull direction of the draft should be reversed. In equivalent terms, whether the draft should use `-angle` as the draft angle (`true`), or `angle` as the draft angle (`false`). Default is `false`.

subfields.name: secondAngle
subfields.description:
Required if `PartingLineSides` is `TWO_SIDED` The second draft angle for a `TWO_SIDED` `PARTING_LINE` draft. Must be between 0 and 89.9 degrees. Note that when using `TWO_SIDED`, `angle` will be applied to faces that are away from the pull direction, from the perspective of the edge. `secondAngle` will be applied to faces that are along the pull direction, from the perspective of the edge. 
 Example: 
`3 * degree`

subfields.name: secondPullDirection
subfields.description: OptionalWhether the pull direction of the draft should be reversed for the second angle of a `TWO_SIDED` draft. In equivalent terms, whether the draft should use `-secondAngle` as the second draft angle (`true`), or `secondAngle` as the second draft angle (`false`). Default is `false`.

subfields.name: tangentPropagation
subfields.description: OptionalFor a `NEUTRAL_PLANE` draft, `true` to propagate draft across tangent faces. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: referenceEdgePropagation
subfields.description: OptionalFor a `PARTING_LINE` draft, `true` to extend the parting line across connected edges of already included faces and tangent faces. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: reFillet
subfields.description: Optional`true` to attempt to defillet draft faces before the draft and reapply the fillets after. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: faceBlend

symbols.name: fillet

symbols.description: Defines the action of a `modifyFillet` feature.

symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Feature performing an `opModifyFillet`.

symbols.name: moveFace

symbols.description: Feature performing an `opMoveFace`.

symbols.name: deripEdges
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, edges is Query)
symbols.description: Splits input sheet metal edges and adjusts them to lie on corresponding sheet metal part faces.

symbols.name: replaceFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Feature performing an `opReplaceFace`.

symbols.name: shell

symbols.name: SplitType
symbols.description: Defines whether a `split` should split whole parts, or just faces.
